class Solution {

public:

    // Encodes a URL to a shortened URL.
    string encode(string longUrl) {
        if (longToShort.count(longUrl)) {
            return base + longToShort[longUrl];
        }
        string key = intToBase62(currentId);
        shortToLong[key] = longUrl;
        longToShort[longUrl] = key;
        currentId++;
        return base + key;
    }

    // Decodes a shortened URL to its original URL.
    string decode(string shortUrl) {
        string key = shortUrl.substr(base.length());
        return shortToLong[key];
    }

private:
    unordered_map<string, string> shortToLong;
    unordered_map<string, string> longToShort;
    string base = "http://tinyurl.com/";
    string chars = "0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ";
    int currentId = 0;

    string intToBase62(int id) {
        if (id == 0) return "0";
        string res;
        int len = chars.length();
        while (id > 0) {
            res.push_back(chars[id % len]);
            id /= len;
        }
        reverse(res.begin(), res.end());
        return res;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:

    // Encodes a URL to a shortened URL.
    string encode(string longUrl) {
        if (longToShort.count(longUrl)) {
            return base + longToShort[longUrl];
        }
        string key = intToBase62(currentId);
        shortToLong[key] = longUrl;
        longToShort[longUrl] = key;
        currentId++;
        return base + key;
    }

    // Decodes a shortened URL to its original URL.
    string decode(string shortUrl) {
        string key = shortUrl.substr(base.length());
        return shortToLong[key];
    }
    
private:
    unordered_map<string, string> shortToLong;
    unordered_map<string, string> longToShort;
    string base = "http://tinyurl.com/";
    string chars = "0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ";
    int currentId = 0;
    
    string intToBase62(int id) {
        if (id == 0) return "0";
        string res;
        int len = chars.size();
        while (id > 0) {
            res.push_back(chars[id % len]);
            id /= len;
        }
        reverse(res.begin(), res.end());
        return res;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1) for decode, O(log N) for encode (where N is the number of encoded URLs)
space complexity: O(N) where N is the number of encoded URLs
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a base-62 encoding scheme with a counter to generate unique short URLs. It maintains two hash maps for O(1) lookups between long and short URLs. The encode method checks for existing mappings to avoid redundant encodings. The base-62 conversion ensures short keys while the counter guarantees uniqueness. Strengths include efficient lookups and minimal storage overhead. Weaknesses include potential key collisions if the same URL is encoded multiple times, though this is mitigated by the longToShort cache.
